title: Deep pothole near Kukatpally flyover causing accidents
description: A 2-foot wide pothole has developed near the Kukatpally flyover after heavy monsoon rains. Multiple two-wheelers have suffered tyre damage and one minor accident occurred this week. Residents have filed complaints multiple times but no action has been taken.

title: Overflowing garbage bin near Miyapur metro station
description: The municipal garbage bin outside Miyapur metro station has been overflowing for the past five days without collection. Waste is spilling onto the footpath and the stench is unbearable for commuters and nearby shopkeepers. GHMC workers have not visited the spot despite repeated calls.

title: Burst water main flooding road in Dilsukhnagar
description: A water main near the Dilsukhnagar bus stand burst three days ago and is continuously flooding the road, making it slippery for vehicles. The HMWS&SB has been notified but a repair crew is yet to arrive. Local businesses are reporting significant losses due to inaccessibility.

title: Broken road stretch damaging vehicles near Tarnaka junction
description: The road surface near Tarnaka junction has collapsed in multiple spots forming large craters. Several cars and bikes have reported tyre damage and suspension issues. The damage has worsened significantly after the recent rains.

title: Pothole-ridden stretch on Mehdipatnam main road
description: The main road through Mehdipatnam near the petrol pump has developed multiple potholes across a 300-metre stretch. Traffic slows to a crawl during peak hours as vehicles weave around the craters. The issue was reported last month and repair work was done but the surface has broken again.

title: Garbage dumped on footpath near Begumpet police station
description: Large quantities of construction and household waste have been illegally dumped on the footpath opposite Begumpet police station. The pile has been growing for over a week and is blocking pedestrian access. Dogs and crows have been scattering the waste further onto the road.

title: Street lights non-functional on Madhapur main road
description: At least eight consecutive street lights on the main road in Madhapur near the Cyber Towers junction have been out for the past two weeks. Pedestrians and cyclists are at serious risk after dark, especially women walking from the IT corridor offices. Locals have submitted a written complaint to the GHMC ward office.

title: Open sewage drain causing health hazard in Uppal colony
description: An open sewage drain near the residential colony in Uppal has been blocked and is overflowing into the street. The stagnant water is breeding mosquitoes and residents fear a dengue outbreak as the area has seen three cases this month. Children playing nearby are at severe health risk.

title: Flooding of residential streets after rain in Bandlaguda
description: Heavy rain on 14 September caused knee-deep flooding in at least six residential lanes in Bandlaguda. Water entered several ground-floor homes and the flooding persisted for over 12 hours due to blocked drains. Residents are demanding a permanent stormwater drainage solution before next monsoon.

title: Pack of aggressive stray dogs attacking pedestrians in Jubilee Hills
description: A pack of approximately 12 stray dogs near Road No. 36 in Jubilee Hills has been attacking joggers and pedestrians in the early morning hours. Two residents have already been bitten and required anti-rabies injections. GHMC's animal husbandry department has not responded despite multiple calls.

title: Faulty traffic signal creating chaos at Panjagutta crossroads
description: The traffic signal at the main Panjagutta crossroads has been stuck on red in one direction for over a week, causing massive congestion during peak hours. Vehicles are jumping the signal as no traffic police are posted. At least three minor collisions have been recorded at this junction.

title: Encroachment of footpath by shop owners in Charminar area
description: Shop owners along the main road near Charminar have permanently encroached upon the footpath by placing display racks and storage crates. Pedestrians are forced to walk on the busy road, creating a dangerous situation especially for elderly visitors and school children. Despite notices, the encroachments have not been removed.

title: Illegal construction without approval in Manikonda layout
description: A builder has begun constructing an unauthorized multi-storey structure in Manikonda without GHMC building permission. The construction is reportedly on storm water drain land, blocking natural drainage of the area. Neighbours have filed a written complaint with the zoning office but work continues unabated.

title: Water leakage from old pipeline in Banjara Hills Road No. 12
description: A pipeline on Road No. 12 in Banjara Hills has been leaking for over ten days, wasting thousands of litres of water daily. The leakage has eroded the road shoulder and is creating a sinkhole risk. Residents contacted HMWS&SB helpline three times with no response.

title: Pothole series on Gachibowli–Nanakramguda road
description: At least 15 potholes have formed on the Gachibowli–Nanakramguda stretch following the monsoon season. IT employees commuting on this road during early morning and late evening are at risk. Two motorcyclists have reported falls in the past two weeks.

title: Ameerpet vegetable market waste not collected for 4 days
description: Vegetable and organic waste from the daily market near Ameerpet metro station has not been collected for four consecutive days. The decomposing waste is emitting a foul smell affecting the metro station and adjacent commercial establishments. Flies and rats have been sighted around the garbage heap.

title: Drainage overflow causing road damage in Nacharam industrial area
description: The main storm drain near Nacharam industrial estate has been overflowing, causing sewage to mix with rainwater and flood the road. The black water is entering factory compounds and workers are wading through it daily. The road surface is also breaking up due to waterlogging.

title: Whole street dark at night in Kondapur residential block
description: An entire residential block in Kondapur near the school has had no functional street lighting for three weeks following a transformer issue. Incidents of chain snatching have increased in the area after nightfall. The welfare association has written to GHMC and TSSPDCL but received no response.

title: Road completely broken near Vanasthalipuram TSRTC depot
description: The road leading to the TSRTC depot in Vanasthalipuram has large cracks and missing asphalt sections spanning about 200 metres. Heavy buses are further worsening the damage with every pass. The route is used by hundreds of schoolchildren daily who are at risk from moving traffic on the damaged surface.

title: Malakpet area garbage not picked up despite complaints
description: Residents of the Malakpet colony have been calling GHMC's 1800 number for six days as garbage has not been collected from their ward. Heaps are accumulating outside each house and on the main lane. Sanitation workers are reportedly on a work stoppage pending wage arrears.

title: Illegal garbage dump near Alwal residential colony
description: Truck drivers are illegally dumping construction debris and municipal waste at a vacant plot near the residential colony in Alwal every night. The dump has grown to alarming proportions and residents believe it is being done at the behest of a local contractor. The smell and health hazard are affecting scores of families.

title: LB Nagar–Hayathnagar road deeply potholed near flyover
description: The stretch of road near LB Nagar flyover on the Hayathnagar side has large potholes that are flooded with water, making it impossible to judge depth. An auto-rickshaw overturned last week injuring three passengers. Locals say the same stretch was patched just six months ago but the surface has deteriorated completely.

title: Sewage overflow on main road in Secunderabad cantonment area
description: A sewage pipe has burst near the Secunderabad Clock Tower and raw sewage is running along the main road. Pedestrians and shopkeepers are unable to use the footpath and the public health risk is severe. The Cantonment Board and GHMC are reportedly in a jurisdictional dispute over who should carry out the repair.

title: Malkajgiri main road broken and waterlogged
description: A 400-metre section of the main road near Malkajgiri railway station is severely cracked and holds standing water even days after rainfall. Bus commuters are struggling to board and alight from vehicles safely. GHMC contractors have been seen at the spot but no repair work has started.

title: Stray cattle blocking traffic near Kothapet signal
description: A herd of six stray cattle has taken up residence near the Kothapet junction and routinely sits in the middle of the road, blocking traffic for extended periods. The animals appear to have been abandoned by a dairy owner. No cattle pound has collected them despite three complaints to GHMC.

title: Night-time dumping of chemical waste in Bowenpally
description: Unknown parties are illegally dumping industrial and chemical waste in a lane behind the Bowenpally wholesale market after midnight. Residents have reported strong chemical odours and one child suffered skin irritation after coming in contact with the waste. CCTV footage has been offered to authorities but no investigation has been launched.

title: Water pipe leaking for weeks in Himayatnagar
description: A HMWS&SB water supply pipe near Himayatnagar main road has been leaking continuously for three weeks, flooding a portion of footpath. Thousands of litres of drinking water are being wasted daily while many residents experience low water pressure. Multiple complaint calls to the water board have gone unresolved.

title: Abids footpath encroached by pan and tea vendors
description: Pan, tea, and snack vendors have permanently erected structures encroaching the entire footpath on the main road at Abids near the bank. Pedestrians including senior citizens cannot use the footpath and must walk on the busy main road. GHMC issued notice two months ago but enforcement action was not taken.

title: Moosapet waste dumped near nala causing blockage
description: Builders are dumping construction rubble directly into the open nala near Moosapet X Roads, narrowing the drain by half. Residents fear that this is why the area floods even during moderate rainfall. The dumping is ongoing and photographs have been shared with local ward members.

title: Traffic signal not working at Nampally station road junction
description: The traffic signal at the T-junction near Nampally railway station has been completely non-functional for over a month. Peak hour traffic near the station has become chaotic and two hit-and-run incidents have been reported. Traffic police are only deployed sporadically and the problem persists.

title: Unauthorized high-rise construction in Kokapet threatens neighbours
description: A developer in Kokapet has begun constructing a G+8 structure without obtaining a building permit or height clearance, dangerously close to existing row houses. Neighbours have reported cracks appearing in their walls due to pile-driving work. A stop-work notice was issued three weeks ago but construction has continued.

title: Nagole underpass flooded blocking commuter route
description: The underpass near Nagole metro station floods to a depth of 3 feet even during moderate rainfall, blocking vehicle access for hours. Two-wheelers were submerged and stalled last week causing a major traffic backup on the Ring Road. The stormwater pump at the underpass has reportedly been non-functional for months.

title: Street light pole dangerously tilted after truck collision in Tolichowki
description: A street light pole near the Tolichowki junction was hit by a goods vehicle and is now leaning at a sharp angle over the road with live wires exposed. Commuters and pedestrians are passing directly under the hazardous pole. Neither GHMC nor TSSPDCL has attended to the emergency despite calls.

title: Garbage piled near Santoshnagar lake affecting water quality
description: Waste from a nearby slum is being regularly disposed of near the Santoshnagar lake bund, with plastic and organic waste sliding into the water body. The lake is visibly turning green with algae and smells foul. Local environmental groups have raised an alarm about the impact on migratory birds that visit the lake.

title: Somajiguda water pipeline leaking near flyover pillar
description: A large water supply pipe running alongside the Somajiguda flyover pillar has a visible crack leaking a continuous stream of water. The water has eroded the shoulder of the service road and poses a risk to the structural integrity of the road base. Passers-by have been photographing the issue and sharing it on social media but no repair crew has arrived.
